Built with doc-gen4, running Lean4. Bubbles () indicate interactive fragments: hover for details, tap to reveal contents. Use Ctrl+↑Ctrl+↓to navigate, Ctrl+🖱️to focus. On Mac, use Cmdinstead of Ctrl.

notation "ℤ" => Int

n "]" => Int.negSucc 
n: Lean.TSyntax `term
n

0: ℕ
0 : ℕ) = (
0: ℤ
0 : ℤ) := 
rfl: ∀ {α : Type} {a : α}, a = a
rfl

ofNat_one: ofNat 1 = 1

1 : ℕ) = (
1: ℤ
1 : ℤ) :=

attribute [local simp] 
ofNat_add_ofNat: ∀ (m n : ℕ), ofNat m + ofNat n = ofNat (m + n)
ofNat_add_ofNat 
ofNat_mul_ofNat: ∀ (m n : ℕ), ofNat m * ofNat n = ofNat (m * n)
ofNat_mul_ofNat 
neg_ofNat_zero: -ofNat 0 = 0
neg_ofNat_zero 
neg_ofNat_of_succ: ∀ (n : ℕ), -ofNat (succ n) = -[1+ n]
neg_ofNat_of_succ
  
neg_neg_ofNat_succ: ∀ (n : ℕ), - -[1+ n] = ofNat (succ n)
neg_neg_ofNat_succ 
ofNat_add_negSucc_ofNat: ∀ (m n : ℕ), ofNat m + -[1+ n] = subNatNat m (succ n)
ofNat_add_negSucc_ofNat 
negSucc_ofNat_add_ofNat: ∀ (m n : ℕ), -[1+ m] + ofNat n = subNatNat n (succ m)
negSucc_ofNat_add_ofNat
  
negSucc_ofNat_add_negSucc_ofNat: ∀ (m n : ℕ), -[1+ m] + -[1+ n] = -[1+ succ (m + n)]
negSucc_ofNat_add_negSucc_ofNat 
ofNat_mul_negSucc_ofNat: ∀ (m n : ℕ), ofNat m * -[1+ n] = negOfNat (m * succ n)
ofNat_mul_negSucc_ofNat 
negSucc_ofNat_ofNat: ∀ (m n : ℕ), -[1+ m] * ofNat n = negOfNat (succ m * n)
negSucc_ofNat_ofNat
  
mul_negSucc_ofNat_negSucc_ofNat: ∀ (m n : ℕ), -[1+ m] * -[1+ n] = ofNat (succ m * succ n)
mul_negSucc_ofNat_negSucc_ofNat

h: m < n

n - m = n - m
Goals accomplished! 🐙

/- ## Quotient and remainder
There are three main conventions for integer division,
referred here as the E, F, T rounding conventions.
All three pairs satisfy the identity x % y + (x / y) * y = x
unconditionally. -/

-- E-rounding (`div` and `mod` defined in core) satisfy 0 ≤ mod x y < natAbs y for y ≠ 0

-- F-rounding: This pair satisfies fdiv x y = floor (x / y)

fdiv: ℤ → ℤ → ℤ

ℤ → 
ℤ: Type

ℤ: Type

0: ℤ
0

m: ℕ
m / 
n: ℕ
n)

m+1:ℕ), -[1+ 
n: ℕ

m: ℕ
m / 
succ: ℕ → ℕ
succ 
n: ℕ
n]
| -[1+ _],

0       => 
0: ℤ
0
